description:
GOAL: Inter-process messaging. send(pid, msg) and receive primitives.
v1 is COPY-ONLY (the zero-copy fast path comes in .19.5).

## Prerequisites

- fz-ul4.19.6 — Atom-table cross-process policy: GLOBAL SHARED
  SINGLETON. Confirms that atom ids in a message are valid in the
  receiver without translation. Deep-copy walker treats atom values
  as opaque scalars; no per-atom rewrite.

## Decisions captured

- Each process owns a Vec<FzValue> mailbox (FIFO).
- send(pid, msg) deep-copies msg into receiver's heap (walks the
  FzValue closure-of-reachability and reallocates in receiver's heap),
  then enqueues into receiver's mailbox.
- receive blocks the current process until a message arrives. v1:
  receive returns the next message (no pattern selectivity; user
  matches at fz level).
- When send delivers to a Blocked process, scheduler marks it Ready.
- Selective receive with pattern matching is a follow-up.
- Receive with timeout is a follow-up.
- Atom values inside messages: shared global ids per .19.6; no
  translation needed.

## Walker refactor (folded in per user decision 2026-05-11, option 6a)

Refactor src/heap.rs::trace() to accept a visitor callback so the
traversal core is shared across:

  - GC mark (existing — visitor marks the object).
  - Deep-copy in this ticket (visitor allocates in destination heap,
    records src->dst forwarding ptr, recursively copies fields).
  - Future closure-of-reachability check for .19.5 (visitor records
    arena membership; bails if it sees a non-arena ptr).

Sketch:

```rust
pub fn walk_heap<V: HeapVisitor>(
    root: FzValue,
    reg: &SchemaRegistry,
    visitor: &mut V,
);

pub trait HeapVisitor {
    fn visit(&mut self, p: *mut HeapHeader, kind: HeapKind) -> WalkDecision;
    // WalkDecision::Recurse | Skip | Stop
}
```

The existing gc::trace becomes a thin adapter over walk_heap with a
MarkVisitor; this ticket adds a CopyVisitor; .19.5 will add an
ArenaCheckVisitor.

## Deliverables

- src/runtime/mailbox.rs: VecDeque-backed mailbox, push_back from
  send, pop_front from receive.
- src/heap.rs refactor: walk_heap + HeapVisitor trait; MarkVisitor
  replaces inline trace logic.
- CopyVisitor (src/runtime/copy.rs or src/heap.rs): traverses FzValue
  closure, reallocs heap objects in target heap. Handles all HeapKinds
  (Struct, List, Bitstring, VecI64/F64/U8/Bit, Map, Closure, Float).
  Cycles + sharing via forwarding-pointer technique: each src object
  is mutated (or a side-table records) src_ptr -> dst_ptr; subsequent
  visits resolve to the existing dst_ptr.
- Builtins: send/2, receive/0. send(pid, msg) signature takes ctx
  (per .11.32), looks up receiver Process in scheduler, runs
  CopyVisitor with receiver.heap as destination, enqueues into
  receiver.mailbox, transitions receiver from Blocked->Ready if
  applicable.
- Scheduler integration: send wakes Blocked receiver via state
  transition + re-enqueue; receive yields if mailbox empty.

## Tests

- Spawn process B; A sends [1, 2, 3] to B; B receives and prints.
  Both processes' heaps see independent copies (assert via heap
  occupancy: both grew by the list size).
- Send a tuple of mixed values.
- Send a deeply nested structure (list of maps of bitstrings) — deep
  copy correctness.
- Send a value with sharing (e.g., a tuple containing the same list
  twice): forwarding-pointer technique preserves sharing in the
  destination (asserted via pointer equality post-copy).
- Send a value with cycles (currently fz has no mutation so cycles
  can't be constructed at user level; if internal cycle ever exists,
  visitor must terminate). Mostly a guard test.
- Mailbox FIFO ordering preserved.
- Receive blocks correctly (process state becomes Blocked); send
  wakes it.
- Atom-identity preserved across send (per .19.6): receiver compares
  received `:ok` to its own literal `:ok` and they're equal.

## Risks

- Deep-copy correctness across all HeapKinds. Mitigation: one test
  per kind + the walker refactor centralizes traversal so all
  consumers stay in sync.
- Sharing collapse: naïve recursive copy duplicates shared
  substructures, blowing up memory. Mitigation: forwarding-ptr
  technique mandatory in CopyVisitor.
- Performance: deep copy is the v1 cost; .19.5 fixes hot cases via
  transferable arenas.

## Out of scope

- Selective receive (follow-up)
- Receive timeout (follow-up)
- Zero-copy send fast path (.19.5)
- Static uniqueness for fast path (.19.7)
title: Mailbox + send / receive (copy fallback)
